title: stachys germanica subsp. germanica
intro: Stachys germanica subsp. germanica, also known as German hedge woundwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting off bacterial and fungal infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin conditions and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Stachys germanica subsp. germanica can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
